rag_rules: Place assume statements in component type scopes when they refer to input ports or features.
Use assign statements in implementation scopes only for output bindings or auxiliary variables.
Generate AGREE annex blocks within annex agree {** ... **}; sections.
generic_rules: Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current component scope.
Distinguish between component type contracts (assumptions/guarantees) and implementation bindings (assign).
Use only named constants or visible features in assumptions and guarantees.
Do not reference AADL properties like Deadline or Period directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts.
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level output bindings.
Constants and equations are declared with `const name : type = value;` and `eq name : type = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables.
Do not invent or copy port/component names not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE variables
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
